name: TAO 4h Asymmetric Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered after directional reset, but trigger, risk, and exit roles are deliberately separated so the trade can survive noise while preserving asymmetric payoff
thesis: Cycle 79 explicitly wants trend continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit indicators play different roles. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only allowed lane with enough trend contamination to justify asymmetric continuation. The research variable is role separation: trigger, risk, and exit must do different jobs instead of collapsing into one indicator stack.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h continuation attempts remain anti-correlated or the asymmetric exits still fail to preserve positive skew, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price resets, reasserts directional control, and continues, while exit logic gives winners more room than losers without using the same indicator for everything.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A repeated failure mode in prior families was indicator role collapse. Separating trigger, risk, and exit functions can preserve edge even when entry is only modestly predictive.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it is the only lane in the basket with enough trend structure for asymmetric continuation to be structurally justified.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Continuation may still be too weak or too late, causing the runner to give back the asymmetry the stop is trying to preserve.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but winners decay, shorten the time-based de-risk step before touching the trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A modest number of continuation attempts, many small wins/losses, and a smaller number of larger asymmetry-carrying trades.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and the separated-role exit structure outperforms simple symmetric exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or a profile where the runner never compensates for stop-outs.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because TAO has enough trend structure for reset/continuation events

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for role-separated asymmetric continuation testing.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough continuation attempts with positive asymmetry from the exit structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h continuation remains too weak or anti-correlated for the asymmetry to matter.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a few larger winners carrying expectancy.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same role-separated continuation survives on the cycle primary transition lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: DOGE 4h preserves enough continuation structure with the same trigger/risk/exit separation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h confirms the concept needs stronger trend contamination.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller asymmetry opportunities than TAO.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to see whether pure chop suppresses the same continuation logic.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints some orderly continuation attempts.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept is too anti-chop there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller continuation carry than TAO.

long: Trail remaining 60% by 0.90 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.2 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 60% by 0.90 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.2 ATR follow-through
